title: Ack.from_bytes lacks maximum bitmap size validation
description:
File: python/src/lichen/schc/fragment.py:129-131

Ack.from_bytes() reads bitmap size n from the packet without bounds checking:
  n = data[2]
  ...
  required_bytes = (n + 7) // 8

If n is a large value (up to 255), required_bytes could be up to 32 bytes. While this is checked against len(body), a malicious or malformed ACK could claim a large bitmap, potentially causing:
1. Allocation of larger-than-expected data structures
2. The subsequent loop (lines 136-138) iterating up to 255 times

Per SCHC spec with 6-bit FCN, max window_size is 62 (MAX_WINDOW_SIZE), so bitmap should never exceed ~62 bits. Values above this indicate a protocol violation.

Fix: Validate n against a maximum (e.g., 64 or MAX_WINDOW_SIZE+1):
  if n > MAX_BITMAP_SIZE:
      raise FragmentError('bitmap size exceeds maximum')

Category: PROTOCOL
